behavior_summary: This script implements the examine ('U8P_LEVER::look') and activation ('use') behaviors for a lever item used in puzzles and environmental interactions.
U8P_LEVER::LEVER::look: when a player inspects the lever it reads the lever's frame and barks either "broken lever " (for frames that indicate broken states) or "lever " otherwise.

U8P_LEVER::LEVER::use: a multi-state controller. Based on the lever's frame (Item::getFrame) and sometimes its quality (Item::getQuality), the lever performs a wide range of game actions: play sound effects, change frames/qualities of itself and many nearby items, spawn/move platform/slider scripts, pop items/NPCs, configure spawned NPCs, toggle or set puzzle-tracking globals (leverTest0..4, makePlatformAppear), call area/spawn routines (TonysBalls), invert the camera, and trigger helper spawned scripts for delayed/cleanup work.
The lever acts on groups of environment objects by enumerating item lists filtered by shape or quality so a single lever activation cascades changes across multiple objects.
notable_control_flow: LEVER::look: simple conditional on Item::getFrame() to choose one of two bark strings and then return.

LEVER::use: branches on the lever's frame value (stored in local [BP-02h]); each branch implements distinct puzzle/environmental effects. Many branches iterate over returned item lists and apply identical operations (Item::setFrame, Item::setQuality, Item::use, spawning slider scripts).
Several branches check and toggle global puzzle flags (leverTest0..4) to gate one-time behaviors and to avoid repeating spawns/effects.
Some branches spawn helper scripts (SLIDER::*, FREE::1C19, METHOD::<id>) and suspend/wait on their return value to serialize multi-step changes (spawn/wait/modify).
Randomized selection is used (urandom + spawned METHOD) for varied NPC/item variants in spawn branches.
open_questions: Exact semantic mapping of lever frame values to named states (which exact frame numbers correspond to 'on', 'off', 'broken', 'special puzzle state').
Precise behavior and effect of TonysBalls (name suggests area effect/spawn but its concrete semantics are inferred).
Which item shapes and quality values are targeted by each enumeration loop (shape constants and their in-game object types are not present in the excerpt).
Exact responsibilities and side-effects of spawned METHOD::<id>, SLIDER::<id>, and FREE::1C19 helper scripts (their roles are inferred from call patterns).
The intended persistence and lifecycle of toggled globals (leverTest0..4, makePlatformAppear): are they intended as permanent puzzle-state flags or temporary session flags?

main_function.name: U8P_LEVER::look
main_function.purpose: Present an examine/description message for a lever item depending on its current frame: emits 'broken lever ' for broken-state frames or 'lever ' otherwise.
entry_conditions: Event 0 / 'U8P_LEVER::look' — triggered when the player (or relevant actor) inspects/examines the lever

function_map.name: U8P_LEVER::look
function_map.role: Handles the 'U8P_LEVER::look' (examine) interaction: read frame and bark an appropriate description.

function_map.name: U8P_LEVER::use
function_map.role: Handles the 'use' (activate) interaction: multi-branch state machine that manipulates environment, spawns entities/scripts, toggles puzzle flags, and provides audiovisual feedback.

external_calls.purpose: Read the lever's animation/frame/state to decide which examine string to show.
Emit the short examine string ('broken lever ' or 'lever ') to the player/process that looked at the item.
Determine the lever's current frame to select the activation branch.
Read lever/item quality to decide sub-branches or filter which items to act upon in loops.
Play sound effects to provide audio feedback for lever activations and associated actions.
Change the lever's and other items' frames to reflect new visual/functional states (open/closed/activated/etc.).
Modify item qualities used as secondary state or filters for subsequent operations.
Invoke an area/spawn/placement routine at coordinates derived from items (inferred to spawn or trigger area effects).
Retrieve Z coordinate of items used when calling TonysBalls or when positioning pops.
Retrieve Y coordinate of items used when calling TonysBalls or when positioning pops.
Retrieve X coordinate of items used when calling TonysBalls or when positioning pops.
Spawn NPCs as part of certain activation branches.
Create/pop items or NPC instances at world coordinates during spawn branches.
Set status bits on spawned items/NPCs to configure initial state immediately after creation.
Generate random numbers to vary selection of spawned variants or to randomize behavior.
Start helper scripts to move sliders/platforms, handle delayed actions, or finalize spawn logic. Code often suspends/waits on their return value.
Toggle or apply a screen invert visual effect for certain branches.
Set the AI target of spawned NPCs so they act immediately (attack player, roam, etc.).
Assign faction/alignment to spawned NPCs.
Mark spawned NPCs as in a combat state when relevant.
Cascade activation to other nearby items by calling their use handlers within loops.
Apply a global/stat bonus (+10 inferred) as a side-effect in at least one branch.

reads.name: makePlatformAppear (global [0244 01])
reads.likely_effect: Gates whether a platform-appearance branch will run; used as a toggle/test to decide platform-spawning behavior.

reads.name: leverTest0 (global [0231])
reads.likely_effect: Used as a puzzle-state marker to prevent or allow a one-time sub-behavior; read to conditionally invoke LEVER::0B34-style logic.

reads.name: leverTest1 (global [0232])
reads.likely_effect: Same class as leverTest0: a per-subpuzzle gate for additional behavior.

reads.name: leverTest2 (global [0233])
reads.likely_effect: Same class as leverTest0: a per-subpuzzle gate for additional behavior.

reads.name: leverTest3 (global [0234])
reads.likely_effect: Same class as leverTest0: a per-subpuzzle gate for additional behavior.

reads.name: leverTest4 (global [0235])
reads.likely_effect: Same class as leverTest0: a per-subpuzzle gate for additional behavior.

writes.name: makePlatformAppear (global [0244 01])
writes.likely_effect: Set to enable platform appearance in some branches (acts as persistent toggle for platform-related puzzle state).

writes.name: leverTest0 (global [0231])
writes.likely_effect: Toggled/cleared/set to record that a lever sub-test/puzzle step has been activated or completed.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the examine ('U8P_LEVER::look') and activation ('use') behaviors for a lever item used in puzzles and environmental interactions.
